entry.title: SAP S/4HANA public cloud : Payment advice not sent to suppliers via email.
entry.content:
Hello,

 

I am working on payment advice to suppliers on sap S/4HANA public cloud

The payment notice form is confiugured through the channels PDF, email and xml

the problem is, when I run automatic payments to the suppliers, the payment advice is generated (via the channels pdf and email) but it is not sent to the supplier (even though when it is generated, it's generated in the language of the BP)

any insights on how to reslove this please? I have tried to add an Email recipient in the Output Parameter Determination app, but still no email is being sent.

Thank you so much.

entry.title: OT Sap Hana para localizações fixas
entry.content: Bom dia, trabalho com sap hana e localizações fixas Como Faço Para gerar ot já que não aparecem na LX11 e nem na LT22.

entry.title: Re: Issue while consuming API, using create_by_url
entry.content: Good, it solves the semicolon error, but now the actual error is that it's an invalid command. Please refer to the documentation of your API, test it using Postman or whatever software.

entry.title: Re: Is integration of C4C with S4/HANA by 3rd party middl...
entry.content:
Hello Adam,

It is possible to use other tools for connecting C4C with a back end system then CPI.

The advantage of CPI is that it offers out of the box iFlows for the objects used - like master data and transactional data. After setting up your communication system and arrangements and your code list mapping you can do the replication between environments very fast (just days 😁).

When working with an other tool defining the interfaces, might take much more time as you will built them from scratch and are therefore not very cost effective. Check all the objects and connections you need, keep any future changes in mind and calculate wisely.

Kr.

MJ

entry.title: Re: "WA_TOP1" cannot be converted to the row type of "IT_...
entry.content:
I'm nitpicking: I guess that REUSE_ALV_COMMENTARY_WRITE has parameter IT_LIST_COMMENTARY of type SLIS_T_LISTHEADER, so I would fix it by keeping IT_TOP1 typed like SLIS_T_LISTHEADER.

DATA wa_top1 TYPE LINE OF slis_t_listheader.
DATA it_top1 TYPE slis_t_listheader.

entry.title: Anyone worked on the integration the reuse component in SAP Fiori element Object?
entry.content:
Hello Gurus,

I have a Fiori element application where when I click on the any row item, its navigate to object page.

Here on the object page I have to display dynamic table based on conditions. [No of column varies]

Now I am trying/tried below option;

1. Create dynamic table on object page - currently working on

2. Reuse component, add reuse component in Fiori element application manifest.- Code failed not working

3. Cross navigation - not getting event on Fiori list report to use navigation.

Please help if any other approach here. I want dynamic table to click of list report row item.

Thanks in advance

Yogesh

entry.title: Re: "WA_TOP1" cannot be converted to the row type of "IT_TOP1". The reverse is a

entry.title: Re: SAP S4 HANA Business Partner (BP) end to end configurations
entry.content: Please  provide path to enable tax conditions - STCD3 ( GST Feild

entry.title: Can't Build Apps and Build Code be activated on the same subaccount?
entry.content:
Hi builders,

I tried to enable Build code to subaccount which had Build apps already.
But It couldn't be enabled like I couldn't make a new project in Build code.

So I tried to make 2 subaccounts, one was with only Build apps and the other one was with only Build code. But each subaccount couldn't work. I could see warning sign when I tried to make service through Booster.

Did anyone suffer from this problem? Or Can anyone from the product team answer this issue?

entry.title: Re: How to improve the processing speed on SAP Business B...
entry.content: No, the custom solutions are not exactly workcenters, you have to log in in the sap cloud application studio with a development user and check if there are any custom developments on your tenant.

entry.title: BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2 extensibility issue
entry.content:
(Please set the tag as "SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Sales" afterwards. That tag was not available at the time of creation, for some unknown reasons...)

Hi experts,

We need to extend the existing and already implemented standard interface between SAP Cloud for Customer and SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

We refer in particular to the creation of an S4 sales order as a C4C quote follow-up document: a custom field has been defined in context Sales Document Header and needs to be mapped in the SCPI flow according to a specific logic.

Unfortunately, we are unable to find the custom field in the service metadata of BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2. We know how the IDoc interface can be extended in S4 OP (KBA 143580) but we are unable to find any documentation for S4HC.

Can you please verify our issue? 

Thank you and regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2 extensibility issue
entry.content:
BAPI:

It is possible to use custom fields in the BAPI BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2. That is the reason why it is also shown in the "Custom Fields" app. Custom fields can only be added using the EXTENSIONIN structure of type BAPIPAREX of the BAPI. For that, custom fields need to be converted into some special format using the helper methods CL_CFD_BAPI_MAPPING -> map_to_bapiparex_single() for header and CL_CFD_BAPI_MAPPING -> map_to_bapiparex_multi() for items.

IDocs:

We have to say key user extensibility is not supported for IDocs.

Question:

We're wondering why you are using BAPI and IDOC instead of OData V2 / V4 or SOAP Services, which are absolutely recommend in Cloud?

entry.title: Re: Unveiling Innovations: My experience at SIT KOLKATA 2024
entry.content: Thank u for your insightful session . Was very interesting and knowledgeable for the HCM team. Looking forward for many more of such sessions regarding Success Factor.

entry.title: Re: FB05 Clearing by Doc number + Line Item
entry.content:
Hi Pablo,

Using FM POSTING_INTERFACE_CLEARING, or program RFBIBL00, it's easy, because you have fields to enter exactly what you need.

Here part of the documentation for program RFBIBL00:

You can also use a BSELP structure to select a specific line item in the form <document no.><fiscal year><line item no.>.

The document number must have 10 characters and the line item 3, and the preceding zeroes should also be entered.

Example: Selecting the third line of document 0000222222 from fiscal year yyyy.

      BSELP-FELDN_1 = "BELNR"
      BSELP-SLVON_1 = "0000222222yyyy003"

In FM POSTING_INTERFACE_CLEARING it must be similar, using fields FTCLEAR-SELFD and FTCLEAR-SELVON.

Unfortunately, I don't think you can achieve the same outcome in manual postings (FB05/F-28/F-53).

I hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Recruiting: New Career Site Job Search and Job Layout
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

I have a question regarding implementing this, as we need to insert a new field in the job requisition related to the location my concern is we need to change the values where we have currently mapping the information about the location state and country. We have here multiple locations, is it possible to post job in multiple locations? 

Thanks a lot.

entry.title: Re: is Odata supported in SAPME 15.5
entry.content:
Hi and thank you for your replay, this note is talking about how to enable Odata in SAP_BW but we use SAP ME , can Odata be used also for SAP ME? if the service SAP Gateway system is exist in Netweaver , how can I find it? or do I need to install  SAP Gateway system in another server?

 

Br,

Vian

entry.title: Re: Problem with CAP localized data
entry.content: Hi, thanks, I have overseen this details. Oops! I have tried it out locally and came up with a way of getting your example work. With some manual work, the `Divsions_texts` can also be explicitly exposed in your BP service. Please have a look at my PR.

entry.title: Re: Unable to connect to ASE 16 with python 3.8 on RHEL8

Can you reproduce the issue locally by the method described in the document below?

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/using-services#mock-remote-service-as-odata-service-node-js

If you can reproduce the issue, then you can provide your project as a reference in the issue you raised.

https://github.com/cap-js/attachments/issues/50

entry.title: Request for Information on SAP ABAP Prerequisites and Guidance for Beginners
entry.content: I recently started working as a trainee for SAP ABAP and have no idea what it is. I would like to know the prerequisites for ABAP, and since I am not good at coding, I would appreciate any insights.

entry.title: Re: Release Strategy for different currencies
entry.content:
Hi I have a query. We have multiple companies in different region with multiple currencies, and we are maintain a sing release strategy class. However, while making an order for SAR(SAUDI ARABIA), SYSTEM IS throwing a translation of NGN(nigeria), infact we have operations in Nigeria and there separate release strategy is there.

We have maintained the release strategy just like above. However system is throwing translation issue for NGN for Saudi Arabia. How can we restrict the NGN translation for an release strategy that will be triggered for nigeria.

entry.title: Re: CL20N All value for release strategy
entry.content:
Hello,

I think you can't put "*" in that characteristic. You have to choose one by one for all purchasing group that you need.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: RAP - Create mandatory fields Popup
entry.content: I did not mean to add them into key. As per my observations, if they are key fields and draft , then the popup used to come.

entry.title: Re: RAP - Create mandatory fields Popup
entry.content: I did not mean to add them into key. As per my observations, if they are key fields and draft , then the popup used to come.

Have you check configuration assigment role/yser to release code for release strategy ? Check if 3rd level maintain as Agent and release code.

entry.title: Re: RAP - Create mandatory fields Popup
entry.content: Hi Sandeep, I am curious how the draft records creation would fail if they are not key fields. Ideally just uuid(managed) would be enough.

entry.title: Re: Hii Expert,I am having issue related to retention mon...
entry.content:
Hello,

1. Is it possbile to cancel the MIRO, GR, and then Edit the PO?

2. If it's not when you do posting MIRO, you can add GL account for retention based on amount of retention, I think it's on debit. When you want to pay retention you have to reclass first to vendor AP, then you can pay.

entry.title: Restrict Currency in Release Strategy Translation
entry.content:
Hi I have a query. We have multiple companies in different region with multiple currencies, and we are maintain a single release strategy class. However, while making an Huge order for SAR(SAUDI ARABIA), SYSTEM IS throwing a translation of error NGN(nigeria), infact we have operations in Nigeria and the are separate release strategy is there.

We have maintained the release strategy just like above. However system is throwing translation issue for NGN for Saudi Arabia. How can we restrict the NGN translation for a release strategy that will be triggered for Saudi Arabia. Nigeria and Saudi are in two separate company codes.

entry.title: Create outbound delivery instead of production material request in EWM material staging process
entry.content:
Hi, Experts.

I try to process Material Staging for production order CO02, production material request send to EWM.

is there any way to send outbound delivery instead of production material request?

【background】-------------------------------------

I would like to unify the outbound process in EWM managed Warehouse.

Since I recognized that subcontracting process and sales order process generate outbound delivery,

I Would like to create outbound delivery in production process as well.

-----------------------------------------------------------

Thank you in advance.

Best regards.

So Adachi

entry.title: Production order quantity proposed during /SCWM/STAGE
entry.content:
Hi, Experts.

I try to process Material Staging for production order in CO02, and Replenishment in /SCWM/STAGE.

Then Warehouse Tasks are created with the quantity defined in the following settings.

/SCWM/PSASTAGE
→Replenishment quantity for parts (30 EA)

   Quantity in Production order ：200
 　→Quantity in Warehouse task1 ：30
        Quantity in Warehouse task2 ：30
        Quantity in Warehouse task3 ：30
         ・・・

I would like to create warehouse tasks with the production order quantity not replenishment quantity.

Quantity in Production order ：200
 　→Quantity in Warehouse task1 ：200

What customization should I look into?

Thank you in advance.

Best regards.

So Adachi

entry.title: Error while create service contract by BAPI (BAPI_CONTRACT_CREATE)
entry.content:
Hello Experts and SAP Community.

I am unable to captured Service related field by using standard BAPI (BAPI_CONTRACT_CREATE) and getting error while create service contract regarding service related field which are not captured. I am drafting the issue and to get input from you, Please refer below given screen-print.

Initial Screen of Standard BAPI: 

after the giving all input , I have given input as “9”(Service Item) in the ITEM_CAT  and 'U" (account assignment category) for the item.

And I did not find a structure to enter a service related entry.

So when I execute the BAPI,I got error message, Please refer the below message for your reference:

Could you please guide/suggest me in this case, If anyone faced the same scenario in past. or Could you please suggest me is there any alternative solution to achieve this target ?

Thanks in advance for your help & support and even the smallest hints that could help me.

--

Thanks & Regards

Rahul Kumar

#https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/to-create-service-contract-me31k-using-bapi-or-fm/m-p/8900668#M1689755 

#https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/bapi-for-service-contract-me31k/m-p/10860659

entry.title: Re: Is integration of C4C with S4/HANA by 3rd party middl...

Do you know, how we can access S4 SOAP services (i.e. for creating business partners) from C4C, when it is not accessible due to VPN connection? What is the best option from a security perspective to make it accessible from C4C? Best regards, Adam

entry.title: Re: How to create a public CAP service?
entry.content:
@ReenaWadhwa 

To access services in a different subaccount than the UI application, you can use a destination of type SAMLAssertion. For instructions on how to set up the connection, watch the YouTube video below:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8ePyQJsmWYA

entry.title: Re: Profit Center Transfer via Stock Transfer Order
entry.content:
Hello,

It's standard sap function. For standard stock transfer 2 steps, the accounting document is on 641 movement type. While 101 only move for qty only. Without doing this you can't see stock in transit in MB52 or MB5T.

entry.title: Re: Enabling your workforce for the future with the new integrated learning experience
entry.content:
Hi,

we have currently a mismatch of programs in the ILX. The case: When an employee registers to a duration-based program with Required Type "REQ", we see different outcomes in the database and reporting on the one hand and its display on the new learning home page. In our case we can't change the required type, as we also use this program in APs. 

Display in ILX: This program shows up in the "invest in myself" bucket, because it is self assigned.

Reporting: In the reports learning plan and learning needs, as well as in the admin view, the program is defined as required type "REQ". For manager reports this is highly misleading, as for managers it might look like, that the program is required to finish - which is cleary not always the case, as it was self assigned.

Is there any way to align this mismatch? Ideally that self assigned programs, do not inherit the "REQ"-Required type of the program, when self assigned.

entry.title: XML to CSV Conversion with each value enclosed in double quotes
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
When converting the Xml Structure to CSV , I am getting the output as below 

000123678|john|jerry|dummy@gmail.com|1234567584
0001236012|lusy|lams|dummy2@gmail.com|3847667584


But i need the output in the below mentioned format each value should be enclosed in double quotes

"000123678"|"john"|"jerry"|"dummy@gmail.com"|"1234567584"
"0001236012"|"lusy"|"lams"|"dummy2@gmail.com"|"3847667584"
Can someone help me with this ,below is the sample input xml payload

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Employees>
   <Record>
      <EmployeeID>000123678</EmployeeID>
      <FirstName>john</FirstName>
      <LastName>jerry</LastName>
      <Email>dummy@gmail.com</Email>
      <Phone>1234567584</Phone>
   </Record>
   <Record>
      <EmployeeID>0001236012</EmployeeID>
      <FirstName>lusy</FirstName>
      <LastName>lams</LastName>
      <Email>dummy2@gmail.com</Email>
      <Phone>3847667584</Phone>
   </Record>
</Employees>

 XML to CSV Converter Configurations

Thanks,
Pavan Kalyan Racha

entry.title: Re: Hii Expert,I am having issue related to retention mon...

entry.title: Re: RAP - Create mandatory fields Popup
entry.content: Sorry I meant mandatory key field! Could also be that it is an immutable field - see V4 specific section "Working with the Create Dialog in POST-Based Create" in https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/topic/b623e0bbbb2b4147b2d0516c463921a0 for the full list of cases where the dialog would show up!

entry.title: Re: Material Master Maintenance
entry.content:
Hello,

Some question you need to consider :

1. Do you need to show report for this with qty ? If yes, maybe you can use batch of serial number and put characteristic with brand, size etc.

2. But I think you will not show this into report. Because you will purchase as asset. So I think you can put specification and detail on asset master. So, 1 material code only.

entry.title: Fiori Elements Analytical table: Column label text VS Colum label text when grouped
entry.content:
Hi gurus,

I have a Fiori Elements App which uses an Analytical table (With an underlying a CDS + Cube + Query). By using annotation @ui.presentationVariant I am able to having a default grouping, which is good, but am experiencing an undesired effect regarding column labels.

There is one field, for which I specified a custom label 'Billing Document' in the Query to overwrite the labels that inherit from the SAP data element.

Also, this field is one of the used for the the default grouping, as follows:

My issue is that when the app runs and the grouping is applied, the label shown in the column (The custom one set in the Query) does not match with the one shown in the Group (The standard one as per the data element) so it is a bit confusing.

Here I'm highlighting in green the other columns used for grouping and where the labels match, and in yellow this case in which the custom label is not being set in the grouping, only in the column.

Does somebody know If I'm missing some additional annotation? I was not able to figure it out yet.

Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Re: Problem with CAP localized data
entry.content: @patricebender thank you for the solution. It's clear now I was over complicating something that is for free thanks to the CDS compiler. For any other having problems with this check the respository and the patch with the small changes.

entry.title: Re: RAP - Create mandatory fields Popup
entry.content: So my two fields that appear in the popup are not key fields at all. They are only fields that are marked as mandatory in the behavior definition. The popup apparently has nothing to do with key fields, as I only have a UUID as a key field, but this is created automatically.

entry.title: Re: RAP - Create mandatory fields Popup
entry.content: My example is not an error, but an example of how I would like it to be. It makes no sense from the workflow that all mandatory fields appear in this popup. The reason is that I have several different object pages. Depending on the selection in the popup, the object page changes and some sections disappear. It therefore makes no sense for all mandatory fields to appear in the popup if they do not exist.

entry.title: Re: Transporting from Dev systems to Quality and Prod sys...

Hi.

Got it Manoel. Thank you for the information.

Thanks & Regards,
Harsh.

entry.title: Re: No data exists for chosen selection in the T-code MC....
entry.content:
Hello,

I think you don't activate info structure for table S031. Please check the config

Logistics-General-->Logistics Information System(LIS) --> Logisgtics Data Warehouse -->Updating --> Updating Control -->  Activate Update

entry.title: Re: How to Retrieve Key Performance Metrics from SAP ASE...
entry.content:
Hey, @sladebe, again thanks for you response, I had checked that sybpydb python library does not support python version greater than python 3.7. And in our architecture we strictly need to run our python script in python version greater than 3.9. Due to this limitation we are using pyodbc library in python. So, in our architecure we can't run sp_monitorconfig 'all' procedure.

While the sp_monitorconfig 'all' procedure, we are getting the follow error:
pyodbc.Error: ('HY000', "[HY000] [Devart][ODBC][Adaptive Server Enterprise]Can't run sp_monitorconfig from within a transaction. (0) (SQLExecDirectW)")

So, can you provide me an solution to this problem without changing our python architecture?

entry.title: to be deleted..
entry.content: ss

entry.title: Re: solman_setup: Managed system Host configuration - Ena...
entry.content: The name resolution (DNS, hosts local file) is also critical to this to work. I had seen situations where multiple restarts of the DAA were necessary in order to operate properly.

entry.title: Re: BTP BAS checking javascript version

entry.title: Re: bapi in fi-ca
entry.content: You can use the bapi BAPI_CTRACPAYMINC_CREATE

Am I missing any library or something ? Attaching the example root application and my application index.html and root stylesheet.

entry.title: Re: RAP - Create mandatory fields Popup
entry.content: Thanks Sandeep for the clarification. Now I agree - mandatory key field / immutable field

entry.title: Re: What you need to know when chosing between Co-deployment or Standalone SAP GTS, edition for SAP
entry.content:
Hi Trond,

Thanks for the information, it really helps understanding the deployment scenarios and different options.

Could you please let me know if it is possible to migrate already existing GTS E4H 2023 SP02 installed on S4HANA Foundation 2022 SP02 to SAP S4HANA 2022 SP02 (On-Prim and/or Private Cloud) system ?

If it is possible, could you guide through the process or to the direction where I can find the technical documentation.

 

Thanks

Hareesh

entry.title: Re: How to Create Return order with reference to Billing...
entry.content: I updated the question to SAP S/4HANA, this will help the expert to find your question easier.

entry.title: Re: Custom fields are now available for "Manage Customer Line Items" and "Manage Supp
entry.content:
Hi Angelika,

What great news! 

Unfortunately, I'm still working on a S/4HANA cloud, private edition 2023 FSP01. Do you know when this will be made possible for private cloud? 

Thanks in advance.

Best regards

Liesbeth

entry.title: Re: How to Retrieve Key Performance Metrics from SAP ASE...
entry.content:
The version of the Python interpreter I was running was 3.9

Re: Can't run sp_monitorconfig from within a transaction

I think that means you have autocommit set to True (automatically run "begin transaction" before each SQL statement, run "commit transaction" after each SQL statement).   As the error message says, that doesn't work with sp_monitorconfig.  Try running it with autocommit set to False (connect with autocommit set to False):

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/33713800/pyodbc-autocommit-does-not-appear-to-work-with-sybase-and-sqlalchemy

entry.title: Issues in Cost Centers: Actual/Plan/Variance report
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

In Cost Centers: Actual/Plan/Variance report, we have the credit and debit values are not matched.  we are using SAP ECC 6.0 

Debit value is more than credit value.

After running CON2 as a month end activity, cost is not 100% allocated in the Cost Center Report. Debit is more then credit value and Over/Under absorption is not getting zero. 

When we check in KSII  Transaction code, error message says that no Activity price is maintained for period 01 – April, 2024.

While checking at KP26 Tcode, we could see that plan activity value is missing. 

Are we missing anything to get the values correctly in Cost Centers: Actual/Plan/Variance report. 

Please advise.

Thank you

Thangaraj Thomas

entry.title: Update new column to paysheet in sap system
entry.content:
Due to changes in paysheet we are required to update the column in SAP too for FICO posting in Hana.

It should be at earning side and Calculation is depended on Days present.

entry.title: Re: hana db with vector in CAP Application
entry.content: thanks. f how to upgrade hana db to QRC 1/2024 release?

entry.title: Re: Install .war file in SAP CPI
entry.content: A requirement to install a .war file for what reason?

entry.title: How to display KF value from one planning level on another (CAPASUPPLY vs PCAPAUSAGE)
entry.content:
Dear all,

This is the example of SAP4 planning area.

I have KF CAPASUPPLY at PERRESLOC level.
I have KF PCAPAUSAGE at PERPRODLOCRESSRC level.

Now I'd like to see how much of the overall resource available capacity is consumed by which product.

So I'd like to bring CAPASUPPLY value at PERPRODLOCRESSRC level.

How can I achieve this since PERPRODLOCRESSRC is much more detailed level. I need this only for KF1 = PCAPAUSAGE / CAPASUPPLY calculation.

 

Thanks,
Thomas

entry.title: New Down Payment Request approval process in sap byd
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts, 

Can you help us what are the steps we should follow , to create New Down Payment Request approval process in sap byd ?

Thanks and Regards, 

Krishna

entry.title: Questions regarding tenant creation (cost and deleting data)

1. Is there an extra charge for requesting a new Production tenant system?

2. If the customer wants to keep the same Production tenant system (same URL), can we reset the current system URL to start the configuration and other setups again?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
Hi @Abhik30251 

it really depends on the role and with this on the solution or product you need to consult about or develop. As you mention HR consultant I would suggest you check out the learning journeys that are targeted for the topic "Human Capital Management" and Lead to a Certification:

Browse SAP Learning Journeys

At the end of each Learning Journey you find the corresponding certification that this journey prepares for.

Kind Regards, Suzanne

entry.title: ByD Purchase order approval based on Value Limit
entry.content:
I've created the following workflow:

And the following Approval Limit setting:

Now I've created a purchase order by EQ 1248 for 30.000. EQ 1248 is the cost centre manager, EQ 1246 is her manager and EQ 1035 is her manager in the organisational structre. 

I don't know what's wrong, but in the case for 30.000 Euro EQ 1246 is not in the approval list.

entry.title: Call a RFC in ABAP RAP save sequence
entry.content:
Hello All, Hello @Andre_Fischer 

Does anyone know if it is possible to call an RFC (using a destination) in the RAP save sequence to persist data in another SAP system?

I tried to call an RFC in the additional_save or save_modified of (un)managed RAP BO, but in all scenarios, the system generates a short dump. Calling a function module using a destination is not allowed as it triggers an implicit commit, which is forbidden.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud and Embedded PP/DS
entry.content:
Hi Arturo,

Understood, no Embedded PP/DS is available in Public Cloud edition.

Do you know if Public Cloud happens to offer a scheduling visualization tool? Simply to see planned/production orders on a timeline and being able to move them around?

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Deleting Folder not possible
entry.content:
Hi friends,

We have an issue where we can't take ownership of a folder to delete it. The button is inactive for this particular folder. The owner column shows "-". It's quite mysterious. We've already tried with full privileges.

Any ideas on how to resolve this?

Kind regards,

Ismail

entry.title: How to 'Undo/Delete' and FPSA submission?
entry.content:
Hi, 

We recently submitted some FPSAs and realise that due to an issue with Govt Gateway, our 1 submission has actually been submitted 4 times!  MEaning, an employees reported values have been increased by 3 times more than we expected.  When using EYU previously, I was sure there was a column for 'undo/delete'.  THis does not seem to be the case for an FPSA?  Do we just have to submit a further, but with -ve's where we previously have +ve'? SAP HANA

entry.title: Concerns regarding GRID SBP upgrade from 19.16 to 19.20
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are planning existing Grid SBP19.16 to SBP 19.20. and we are following attached readme "README19V2P_PHONE_NUMBER9 2" for the SBP 19.20.
And that Grid is used other for DBs in the same server. So upgrading sbp will lead to downtime for all the four DBs right.

So we have few concerns below,
1. Which backups to be taken before going ahead with the upgrades? (Please mention necessary backups /config backups we should take)
2. Also as a precautionary measures, do we have to take full DB backup of all the four DBs which are connected to GRID and DB GRP also to be taken? Kindly suggest.
3. In case of backout scenario what step to be followed exactly to back to 19.16. If we take any filesystem backup, will it be helpful? And while following the README the 3 number para says, to install the previous sbp. Can you help me on the steps?


Thanks,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Concerns regarding GRID SBP upgrade from 19.16 to 19.20
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Please find below the answers:

1. Please take full database backup with BR*tools.

2. Yes.

3. Filesystem backup is not enough, please take full backup with BR*tools.

To revert to a previous, older SAP Bundle Patch, follow the installation instructions of that older SAP Bundle Patch.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: "WA_TOP1" cannot be converted to the row type of "IT_...
entry.content:
You declared 

it_top1 TYPE TABLE OF slis_t_listheader,

So it_top1 is a table of tables of records of structure slis_listheader (slis_t_listheader is already a table type definition)

entry.title: Re: Attachment deleted when HR Admin updating
entry.content:
Hi,  

does HR Admin has the permission to see/edit the attachment within their permission role?

Is the Workflow approval step set up with "Respect permission = yes".

Is there any business rule in place, which deletes the attachment in case of adding a record to personal information?

In case the permissions and configuration are set up correct, the attachment is not getting deleted within the workflow. Tested in an SAP Demo instance.

 

BR,

Sandra

entry.title: FKKBIX: error FKKBIX2537 during the creation of a BIT2 (cost posting)
entry.content:
COST Postings configuration is setup in the system along with activating the relevant Interface components for BIT class. When we are trying to create BIT using the COST type & Cost subtype we defined in system, the Revenue BIT is failing (going to Raw status) & the respective COST BIT per configuration made is not creating.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: FKKBIX: error FKKBIX2537 during the creation of a BIT...
entry.content: Check your settings in customizing table VC_TFK8260. The cost type OBC is not maintained, but BITTYPE OBC is used for the BIT2. The cost type and the BITTYPE must be the same. Just read F1 help of the field 'Type of Billable Item' in VC_TFK8260.

entry.title: How to Test Your Solution for Breakage
entry.content:
Browsers Are Ending Third-Party Cookie Support

As you might be aware already, browser manufacturers are ending support of third-party cookies. Google Chrome is the most prominent example, with its third-party cookie deprecation program planned for the end of 2024. This might impact your SAP solutions especially regarding session management and single sign-on authentication, as we mentioned in the first installment of our blog series. If you haven’t already, we recommend reading through it to know whether your solution is affected by the third-party cookie deprecation.

In our second installment, we’ll take you through the timeline communicated by Google, and provide you with recommendations to prepare, test for breakage, and minimize the impact on your SAP solution.

 

What is the Timeline?

Here is a timeline with the most important dates you should have in mind:

  • July 2024: End of the grace period.
  • January 2025: Third-party cookie deprecation ramp-up to 100% of all users.

Why Testing Is Important?

Cookies are used for various purposes in many applications. From session information to technical authentication flows, there are many ways an application might be affected from the deprecation of third-party cookies. Thus, we highly recommend you thoroughly test the behavior of your application.

SAP recommends following a three-steps approach:

  1. Test for breakage after the end of the grace period, starting July 1st 2024.
  2. Test for breakage starting January 2025, when third-party cookie deprecation is fully rolled out.
  3. If needed, audit your solution for third-party cookies.

Because SAP has secured deprecation trial tokens for the major domains used by SAP BTP, it is likely that your application will run fine until the end of the deprecation trial period. We recommend making sure that your application continues to run smoothly, even after the temporary measures run out by testing with specific configurations.

These testing steps should give you an idea of how your solution could be affected by the third-party cookie deprecation. We strongly recommend getting a deprecation trial token if your solution is affected, as it will not only allow you to keep things running smoothly until the end of the grace period, but also to prepare you for proper testing before the third-party cookie deprecation is fully rolled out by January 2025. Additionally, this will buy you some time to think of a solution recommended by Google, like CHIPS or Storage Access API (SAA) before the end of the grace period.

We have come up with a proper testing guide that should help you get started with testing. For more information and the full detailed procedure, see Preparing and Testing Your Solution for Third-Party Cookie Deprecation on the help portal.

 

What Should You Do After Testing?

If your notice a breakage after testing, consider Google’s deprecation trial period, during which you can opt-out of the third-party cookie deprecation, request for additional time to migrate away from third-party cookie dependencies, investigate on potential solutions for your application and implement a final solution. If eligible, Google will provide you with a deprecation trial token. We will provide more info on the deprecation trial tokens in an upcoming blog.

This blog is the second part of our blog series covering the third-party cookie deprecation:

  • Are You Affected by the Third-Party Cookie Deprecation? 
  • How to test your solution for breakage. Full testing guide is now available: Preparing and Testing Your Solution for Third-Party Cookie Deprecation .
  • How to handle deprecation trial tokens. (Coming Soon)
  • How to prepare your application for the blockage of third-party cookies. (Coming Soon)

Keep watching this space for more information regarding Google’s third-party cookie deprecation.

entry.title: Re: SQL Queries with Service Layer returns error code 402
entry.content:
We had the same problem after updating from fp2110 to sp2402. We did this (from troubleshooting note 3064927 - this part is same both on linux and windows, different folders):

    • The issue could be related to corrupted SQLQueries config file. Goto ServiceLayer Installation folder (%ProgramFiles%\SAP\SAP Business One ServerTools\ServiceLayer\Conf\).
    • Rename the file b1s_sqltable.conf to b1s_sqltable_backup.conf
    • Open b1s_sqltable.template.conf and save as b1s_sqltable.conf.
    • Restart the Service Layer Service.

After that b1s/v1/SQLQueries worked well, but we had to manually edit the b1s_sqltable.conf file and add some tables that we needed for our queries.

entry.title: Multi-tier Collaborative Outsourcing Manufacturing Process with SAP Business Network
entry.content:
Why Outsourcing Manufacturing Process

Outsourcing manufacturing processes offers several benefits that can enhance a company's operational efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and overall competitiveness.

  1. Cost Savings:
  • Lower Labour Costs: Outsourcing to countries with lower labour costs can significantly reduce production expenses.
  • Reduced Overhead: Outsourcing eliminates the need for investment in expensive machinery, factory space, and other infrastructure.
  1. Focus on Core Competencies:
  • Companies can concentrate on their core business activities, such as research and development, marketing, and sales, while leaving the manufacturing to specialized firms.
  1. Access to Expertise and Technology:
  • Outsourcing partners often have specialized skills, advanced technologies, and industry expertise that can improve product quality and innovation.
  1. Scalability and Flexibility:
  • Companies can quickly scale production up or down in response to market demand without the constraints of fixed costs associated with owning and operating manufacturing facilities.
  1. Risk Mitigation:
  • Outsourcing can spread risk by diversifying the supply chain, reducing the impact of localized disruptions.
  • It also reduces the financial risk of investing in manufacturing facilities and technology that may become obsolete.
  1. Faster Time to Market:
  • Specialized manufacturers can often produce goods more quickly due to their expertise and streamlined processes, speeding up time-to-market for new products.
  1. Access to Global Markets:
  • Outsourcing can provide access to international markets, enabling companies to establish a global presence and reach customers more effectively.
  1. Regulatory Compliance:
  • Outsourcing partners, particularly those with experience in international markets, are often well-versed in regulatory requirements, helping companies comply with local laws and standards.
  1. Capital Conservation:
  • By avoiding the large capital expenditures associated with building and maintaining manufacturing facilities, companies can conserve capital for other strategic investments.
  1. Enhanced Supply Chain Management:
  • Outsourcing can lead to more efficient supply chain management by leveraging the expertise of specialized firms in logistics and supply chain optimization.

Multi-tier Collaborative Outsourcing Manufacturing Process with SAP Business Network

Multi-tier Collaborative Outsourcing Manufacturing on SAP Business Network involves coordinated efforts across multiple tiers of suppliers and manufacturers, enhancing visibility, communication, and efficiency throughout the supply chain. This approach leverages the integration of SAP Business Network with SAP S/4HANA to facilitate seamless collaboration and optimize the entire production process.

  • Provide CMO’s with near real time visibility into demand forecast.
  • Enable CMO’s to commit to buyer’s requested forecast quantities to manage constrained planning process.
  • Visibility for CMOs WIP (Work-in-progress) Inventory data, Component inventory visibility ,  drop shipment process between Component Supplier and CMO etc.
  • Management of PO Batch # Assignment for batch genealogy, Earlier visibility into batch quality and results
  • Enables contract manufacturers to report receipt and consumption of components with relevant batch information or Serial no information as relevant.

Processes Steps

Planning Process

  • The Planner of the Buying Organization perform the planning for FG to be produced according to the planning cycle/ horizon. During the planning process, considering the In-house plant capacity and available resources planner take decision on In-house production as well as forecast the allocated Quantities to be manufactured by the by the CMO (Contract Manufacturers) in SAP IBP.
  • The Contract Manufactures can provide the commitment to the Forecast depending on available capacity. They can also provide upside/downside capacity availability on SAP Business Network.
  • The Planner of the buying organization can re-plan and adjust the allocation as per CMOs forecast commitments and capacity availabilities in SAP IBP.

Multi-tier Purchase Order for Components Process

  • The BOM (Bill of Material) explosions for the forecast committed Contract Manufacturing plan / quantity of FG (Finished Goods) is performed and the Quantity of each components required is derived. Based on availability of Inventory of the components, the multi-tier purchase order for components is released to the component suppliers and copy is shared with CMO for transparency and visibility.
  • The Component supplier would be directly shipping the components to the CMO location / plant as mentioned in the multi-tier purchase order.
  • The CMO would receive the components against the Multi-tier Component Purchase order on Business Network and corresponding receiving document would be created automatically in Buyer’s SAP S4HANA by giving visibility component inventory present at the CMO premise.

Copy Supplier – Multi-tier Purchase Order in SAP S4HANA

Drop Ship the Components to Directly to CMO

Multi-tier PO Visibility to CMO as well as Components supplier on SAP Business Network

Components Receipt by CMO referencing to ASN created by Componenet Supplier

Component Inventory at CMO Premise on SAP Business Network

Sub-Contracting Purchase Order for FG production to CMO.

  • The buyer would create a Sub-Contract Purchase Order (Item Type L) for manufacturing of FG (Finished Good) product to the CMO. The Sub-contract order would have BOM details / components required for production / assembly of FG product.
  • The CMO would start production process steps / assembly or fabrication process steps. CMO can share the WIP – Work-in-progress Inventory details using Manufacturing visibility functionality of SAP Business Network
  • The CMO can report the Realtime Component consumption details during the FG production processes. The SAP business network support both real time ( WIP level) consumption posting as well as Backflush scenario ( all Component consumption posting at the end of FG production).
  • This process provides component consumption tracking , Component inventory & WIP inventory visibility and reconciliation of Components etc.
  • During this process, batch genealogy or Serial number tracking are maintained as necessary. For perishable items, the self-life can be tracked too.
  • The Delivery of FG with ASN process as well as digital invoice submission for CMO is supported on SAP Business Network.

Sub-contracting PO on SAP Business Network

Component Consumption Reporting by CMO

Integrations between S4HANA and SAP Business Network for Sub-Contracting Process

Integrating SAP S/4HANA with SAP Business Network for the subcontracting process using the Cloud Integration Gateway (CIG) involves below steps.

References :

Explaining Subcontracting Collaboration

Demonstrating the Subcontracting with Multi-Tier Collaboration Process

Component Planning Collaboration with SAP Business Network

entry.title: Risk class update in mass from BAPI or any other tool for BP transaction
entry.content: I need to update Risk class in mass under Credit Profile Tab in BP transaction. Do we have any BAPI or tool which can be helpful to update for BP customers.

entry.title: Explore and Infuse DOX capabilities in SAP BTP custom applications
entry.content:
Introduction to Document Information Extraction

Document Information Extraction (a.k.a DOX) comes to rescue when we need to process large amounts of business documents that have content in headers, line items and tables. Extracted information can be further used, for example, to automatically process payables, invoices, or payment notes while making sure that invoices and payables match. After uploading a document file to the service, it returns the extraction results from header fields and line items. Leverage the power of Generative AI to automate the information extraction with the DOX Premium edition.

Plan offerings for DOX 

Base plan: Base Edition service plan includes all core features but doesn't include document information extraction using generative AI. 

  • Service plan intended for productive usage. 
  • Inference requests in blocks of 100 documents and compute hours. 
  • We can upload to the service up to 2000 documents per hour per tenant (each document can have up to 100 pages). 

Premium: Premium edition service plan that includes document information extraction using Generative AI. 

  • Service plan intended for productive usage. 
  • Inference requests in blocks of 100 documents. 
  • We can upload to the service up to 2000 documents per hour per tenant (each document can have up to 100 pages)

Note: There is Free and trial offering as well. For this blog, we will be using a trial plan.

For Metering and Licensing, refer this 

Supported Document types and formats 

  • Invoice 
  • Business Card (only via API) 
  • paymentAdvice 
  • purchaseOrder 

Document Information Extraction supports the following document file formats as input: 

  • Document files in PDF format 
  • Single-page document files in JPEG, PNG, and TIFF format 
  • Image files that include scene text in JPEG, PNG, and TIFF format 
  • E-invoice document files in PDF and XML hybrid format, and in Factur-X and ZUGFeRD standards (all versions) 
  • payment Advice document files in Excel format 

For information related to supported languages and regions, refer this.

What are the DOX benefits? 

With Document Information Extraction we can: 

  • Process documents more efficiently with fewer errors and difficulties. 
  • Increase quality and compliance mechanisms. 
  • Reduce the time required to process a document. 
  • Allow your organization's members to focus on more relevant tasks in their expertise
  • Leverage Generative AI capabilities with Premium edition to automate document processing

My use case scenario 

The requirement is straightforward, we want to extract information from SAP standard documents (Invoice, Purchase, Sales Orders etc.) or even custom documents like taxi expense receipts for further processing. 

Using the Document Information Extraction UI is much easier than API based usage, hence the objective of this blog is to provide an overview of DOX APIs and how to leverage them in custom applications build to enable the OCR capabilities.

DOX architecture(use case)

 

If you wish to explore Document Information Extraction UI then follow this tutorial

Prerequisites 

1) SAP Document Information Extraction (Trial account): Refer this tutorial for spinning off free DOX trial account 

2) SAP BTP trial account: Refer this tutorial for setting up a free BTP trial account for UI5 application development

In order to explore and consume DOX APIs, first, an service key needs to be created, to authenticate and authorize the incoming requests. Please refer this tutorial to generate a service key from instance 

Let's walkthrough and understand the DOX API's first via an HTTP tool(Postman) and then we jump into BTP UI5 application development part. Using the DOX APIs, we will be able to extract documents information in just 3 steps.

 

1. Get OAuth access token

Below are the mandatory parameters which needs to be entered to generate an access token successfully.

 

OperationGET
URLhttps://{{uaa url from the DOX service key}}/oauth/token?grant_type=client_credentials
Query Params 
grant_type=client_credentials
AuthorizationUsername: clientid from the DOX service key
Password: clientsecret from the DOX service key

Access token call

Note: Access token validity is for 12hours

 

2. Upload/Post document call

Let's take a simple Invoice document pdf with few reference fields we need to extract.

Sample Invoice Document

 

HeaderFields: purchaseOrderNumber,netAmount,senderAddress,currencyCode

LineItemFields: description,netAmount,quantity,unitPrice,materialNumber,unitOfMeasure

 

OperationPOST
URLhttps://{{Backend endpoint url from the DOX service key}}/document-information-extraction/v1/document/jobs
Form-Datafile= {{Upload document file with supported extension}}
Form-Data

options=

{
"extraction": {
"headerFields": [
"purchaseOrderNumber",
"netAmount",
"senderAddress",
"currencyCode"
],
"lineItemFields": [
"description",
"netAmount",
"quantity",
"unitPrice",
"materialNumber",
"unitOfMeasure"
]
},
"clientId": "default"
}
AuthorizationBearer {{Access Token generated from the first call}}

Upload document call

Under options parameter, we need to key in a JSON object which contains reference header and line item fields we need to extract from the document, enrichment parameters (if any), clientId (The ID of the client, this is a mandatory field. For trial account it is default but we can create our own client using Client API)

Note: Detailed optional parameters are well explained in the API reference guide

Upon successful operation of upload document call, the status of the job will be in PENDING status which means the document is uploaded successfully and currently being processed, we can store the request Id which will be further used to get the DOX extraction results.

 

3. Get Extraction results

Extraction results can be retrieved by calling the jobs API with the request id retrieved after the upload call. Once the document processing is successful the status for extracted document will be in "DONE" status.

 

OperationGET
URL

https://{{Backend endpoint url from the DOX service key}}/document-information-extraction/v1/document/jobs/{{document id from the POST call response}}

AuthorizationBearer {{Access Token generated from the first call}}

 

Extract document results call

 Header section extracted fields

 

In the extract results response, header and line items fields will be separated and there is also an extraction confidence level against each field. (This can be used to determine the accuracy of extraction and if needed a manual check step can also be performed in case of automation scenarios)

Now, we have explored and understand the DOX APIs functionality via Postman, it's time to infuse the DOX APIs in BTP UI5 application. 

Infuse DOX APIs in SAP BTP UI5 Application

For the sharing purpose, we have build one lightweight UI5 application via Business Application Studio and deployed it on SAP BTP. It takes the input from user to upload the document(Invoice in our case) as an attachment and once the document is uploaded, backend code will infuse the DOX APIs via BTP Destinations to perform the OCR operation.

BTP Destinations: used to store and retrieve any technical information which is required to connect to a remote service(DOX APIs in our case) from the application deployed in BTP. 1st destination is to get the access token and other destination to call DOX APIs.

Destination for token

Destination for DOX APIs

Once the destinations are maintained, we can proceed with the Business Application Studio to start building the UI5 application. Below is the code snippet which takes the upload file as input, push it to DOX APIs for the document upload call and then finally extracts the results out of the completed document job.

 

 

onUpload: function () {
                var that = this;

                var oFileUpload = this.getView().byId("fileUploaderScanner");
                var oUploadedFile = oFileUpload.oFileUpload.files[0];
                const blob = new Blob([oUploadedFile], { type: oUploadedFile.type });

                var oOptions = {
                    extraction: {
                        headerFields: ["purchaseOrderNumber", "netAmount", "senderAddress", "currencyCode"],
                        lineItemFields: [
                            "description",
                            "netAmount",
                            "quantity",
                            "unitPrice",
                            "materialNumber",
                            "unitOfMeasure"
                        ]
                    },
                    clientId: "default"
                };

                var oFormData = new FormData();
                oFormData.append("options", JSON.stringify(oOptions));
                oFormData.append("file", blob, oUploadedFile.name);

                var oScanModel = new JSONModel();
                oScanModel.loadData("oauth/token", "grant_type=client_credentials", {
                    "Content-Type": "application/json"
                });
                oScanModel.attachRequestCompleted(
                    function (oData) {
                        var sAccessToken = oData.getSource().getProperty("/access_token");

                        var oHeaders = {
                            Accept: "application/json",
                            "X-Requested-With": "XMLHttpRequest",
                            Authorization: "Bearer " + sAccessToken
                        };

                        const requestOptions = {
                            method: "POST",
                            headers: oHeaders,
                            body: oFormData
                        };

                        fetch("document-information-extraction/v1/document/jobs", requestOptions)
                            .then((response) => response.text())
                            .then((result) => {
                                var oResult = JSON.parse(result);
                                var sJobId = oResult.id;

                                    fetch("document-information-extraction/v1/document/jobs/" + sJobId, {
                                        headers: {
                                            Authorization: "Bearer " + sAccessToken
                                        }
                                    })
                                        .then((response) => response.json())
                                        .then((result) => {
                                            var oNewClaimModel = new JSONModel({
                                                PONumber: result.extraction.headerFields.find(
                                                    (x) => x.name === "purchaseOrderNumber"
                                                ).value,
                                                Vendor: result.extraction.headerFields.find(
                                                    (x) => x.name === "senderAddress"
                                                ).value,
                                                Amount: result.extraction.headerFields.find(
                                                    (x) => x.name === "netAmount"
                                                ).rawValue,
                                                Receipt: result.fileName
                                            });
                                            that.getView().setModel(oNewClaimModel);
                                        })
                                        .catch((error) => console.error(error));
                            })
                            .catch((error) => console.error(error));
                    }.bind(this)
                );
            },

 

 

Final outcome: Once the UI5 application is deployed, below is the user input screen which takes the document as input and once it is submitted, the backend code will call the DOX APIs in below sequence.

1) Access token will be fetched

2) Submitted document will be uploaded in the /documents DOX API,

3) Once the document is processed by DOX(probably within few seconds) then extractions results can be fetched via Job Id (as explained in Postman testing section)

4) After getting the extraction results, relevant fields can be mapped to appear in the application UI

 

Invoice Submission UIUpload document UI

Extraction results UI

 

With this blog, we have explored the DOX capability(powered by Generative AI) and how to infuse the DOX APIs for any custom scenarios where OCR is required.

 

Happy Extracting!! 🤖

 

Spoiler
In case, you want to infuse DOX within any Automation or Workflow scenario then SAP Build Process Automation service also offers Document Information Extraction SDK. Refer this page to explore more about DOX in SAP Build Process Automation.

Important links to consider:

1) API reference with common error codes and status: Link

2) Limitations and technical constraints: Link

3) SAP help for DOX: Link

Disclaimer: Above post is purely based on the personal learning and to showcase the basics of how we can leverage DOX APIs for the OCR requirements. Feel free to explore more and improvise your developments as per your particular use cases. Happy to hear comments and feedback.

entry.title: Re: Transport Error (after-import method RS_ODSO_AFTER_IM...
entry.content:
What is the error message ID and Number for the empty error lines?

Does it also errors out when you manually activate the DSO?

entry.title: Re: Hello world
entry.content:
@Chandan...The SAP Digital Supply Chain is a great area to focus on from a job and career standpoint. At a very high level I would look into how it's used... integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud... SAP Business AI.... SAP Intelligent Spend & Business Networks... and Sustainability (Environmental) aspects.

A few places to check out...
- SAP Supply Chain Management (sap.com) (aka Digital Supply Chain - DSC)
- DSC Customer Stories
- Innovating for resilient and sustainable supply chain management
- SAPinsider: SAP Supply Chain Management
- SAP User Group(s) - DSAG (German SAP User Group)

The more you look... the more you'll find... As a post-graduate degree student... Ask yourself how you would apply what you see to real-life customer situations... Connect with SAP DSC professionals for informational discussions. You're on the right track!:-)

entry.title: Re: SAP CPQ BOM not exploded
entry.content: Hi @Fancy_Zou, thank you for your hints. We now could on that information solve this problem. The wrong plant was used when creating the KB in the ERP and therefore no explosion was possible and no bomItem Attribute was available in CPQ. Now it works like expected .

entry.title: Re: ADSO fields getting empty post data activation
entry.content: I would suggest to first really check the KBA that was provided by @MKreitlein. Also ensure, that before and after image pairs of the same key are processed within the same package. Otherwise it can happen that the before image is processed on it's own, which leads to the initialization of data fields.
